title: pittosporum lenticellatum
intro: Pittosporum lenticellatum, also known as Kohuhu, is a small, deciduous tree native to New Zealand and found in the subtropical regions of Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pittosporum lenticellatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum lenticellatum can be made into infusions, teas, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
